audit.method: In-app Browser CDP, Accessibility tree, probes.js, desktop/mobile interaction checks
scopeNotes: Single-page homepage sample; AAA and removed criterion 4.1.1 are excluded.
In-app Browser keyboard delivery and 320px screenshot capture were unavailable; related checks remain open.
No authenticated or multi-step process was in scope.

summary.note: The sampled homepage is not Level AA conformant because six Level A/AA failures were found.

toPass.1.4.12: Text-spacing override did not produce horizontal scrolling.
toPass.2.5.8: Targets below 24px met the required spacing exception.
toPass.mobile-menu: The labelled mobile menu button opened a labelled mobile navigation region.

toFail.1.4.4: 200% zoom produced horizontal scrolling and offscreen navigation/content.

issues.understanding: At 200% text zoom, content and functionality must remain available without horizontal scrolling.
issues.summary: The desktop header and hero overflow horizontally at 200% zoom.

instances.issue: Viewport width was 1279px while document scrollWidth was 2212px.
instances.evidence: evidence/wcag-homepage-zoom-200.png; probes.js overflow() returned horizontalScroll: true.

issues.userImpact: People who enlarge text can lose navigation choices and key hero content.

remediation.what: Make navigation and hero layout respond to enlarged text without fixed/minimum widths.
remediation.codeHint: Use wrapping or the mobile menu before horizontal pressure occurs; replace fixed/translated width patterns with max-width: 100%, min-width: 0, and responsive grid/flex rules.
acceptanceCriteria: At 200% zoom, document scrollWidth does not exceed viewport width except for exempt content.
All header links and contact CTA remain available without horizontal panning.

issues.understanding: Content must reflow at 320 CSS pixels without two-dimensional scrolling.
issues.summary: The page overflowed 50px horizontally at the Browser's effective 305px mobile viewport.

instances.issue: Document scrollWidth was 355px at a 305px viewport.
instances.evidence: Mobile probe data: menu button right edge 355px; main content right edge 347px.

issues.userImpact: Mobile and high-zoom users must pan sideways to reach content and controls.

remediation.what: Eliminate the overflow source and ensure responsive containers are constrained to the available inline size.
remediation.codeHint: Audit mobile x-offsets/margins and any min-width values; use width: 100%, max-width: 100%, box-sizing: border-box, and min-width: 0 on flex/grid children.
acceptanceCriteria: At 320px CSS width, documentElement.scrollWidth equals clientWidth for ordinary page content.
The mobile menu control and all main content fit within the viewport.

issues.understanding: Meaningful images need an equivalent text alternative; decorative images need an empty alternative.
issues.summary: The hero background image has no alt attribute.

instances.issue: Next.js emitted an Image is missing required alt property error.
instances.evidence: Source was /images/home/hero-background.png; probes.js imagesNoName included this element; browser console logged the missing-alt error.

issues.userImpact: Screen-reader users may receive incomplete or noisy treatment of the hero imagery.

remediation.what: Supply the image's purpose or explicitly mark it decorative.
remediation.codeHint: Add alt='meaningful description' when the image communicates content; otherwise set alt='' on the Next Image component.
acceptanceCriteria: The console no longer reports a missing alt property.
The image has an accurate alternative or alt='' when decorative.

issues.understanding: Heading and section relationships must be programmatically determinable.
issues.summary: The page jumps from its h1 to h3 service-card headings and exposes several empty h2 headings.

instances.issue: Heading structure is incomplete and contains unnamed entries.
instances.evidence: probes.js recorded h1 -> h3; Accessibility.getFullAXTree and DOM snapshot included multiple heading [level=2] nodes with no accessible name.

issues.userImpact: Screen-reader users navigating by headings receive an unclear page outline.

remediation.what: Use a coherent hierarchy and remove empty structural headings.
remediation.codeHint: Add descriptive h2 headings before related h3 groups; convert empty h2 elements to non-heading containers when there is no section heading.
acceptanceCriteria: No heading is empty in the accessibility tree.
Section h3 headings have a preceding meaningful h2 parent section.

issues.understanding: Normal-size text requires at least 4.5:1 contrast against its background.
issues.summary: Article and Perspective labels are 2.91:1 green-on-white text.

instances.issue: Small accent metadata text is below the AA contrast threshold.
instances.evidence: Foreground rgb(32,174,73) on white; 12px text; calculated ratio 2.91:1.

issues.userImpact: People with low vision or reduced contrast sensitivity can miss article categories.

remediation.what: Use a darker text color for accent metadata on white.
remediation.codeHint: Introduce a darker --accent-text token that is at least 4.5:1 against white, or apply a compliant existing text token to these labels.
acceptanceCriteria: Article and Perspective labels calculate to at least 4.5:1 on their rendered background.

issues.understanding: Headings must describe their topic or purpose.
issues.summary: Several level-2 headings have no accessible name.

instances.issue: Empty h2 nodes appear in the accessibility tree.
instances.evidence: Accessibility snapshot lists multiple heading [level=2] entries without names.

issues.userImpact: Heading shortcuts announce blank entries, making page navigation confusing.

remediation.what: Give every retained heading a concise, visible, accessible label.
remediation.codeHint: Populate the text content from Sanity-backed section data; remove heading tags used only for layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Every h2 has a non-empty descriptive accessible name.
Heading navigation contains no blank stops.

needsReview.title: Keyboard
needsReview.whatToReview: Operate every link and the mobile menu with Tab, Enter, and Space.
needsReview.whyOpen: The in-app Browser did not advance focus with Tab and raw CDP keyboard input is unsupported.
needsReview.howToConfirm: All functionality must be usable with keyboard alone.

needsReview.title: No Keyboard Trap
needsReview.whatToReview: Tab into and out of the mobile menu and all interactive regions.
needsReview.whyOpen: A complete focus walk was unavailable.
needsReview.howToConfirm: Focus can always move onward or exit with a standard keyboard method.

needsReview.title: Pause, Stop, Hide
needsReview.whatToReview: Inspect the 19 detected infinite animations for visible moving content.
needsReview.whyOpen: The probe cannot establish whether each animation is visible or user-controllable.
needsReview.howToConfirm: Any visible movement lasting more than five seconds has a pause, stop, or hide mechanism.

needsReview.title: Focus Order
needsReview.whatToReview: Check complete Tab order at desktop and mobile widths.
needsReview.whyOpen: No focus sequence could be captured.
needsReview.howToConfirm: Focus follows a meaningful visual and interaction order.

needsReview.title: Multiple Ways
needsReview.whatToReview: Verify a second page-discovery mechanism beyond primary navigation.
needsReview.howToConfirm: Representative pages can be found through navigation plus search, sitemap, or another independent mechanism.

needsReview.title: Focus Visible
needsReview.whyOpen: Tab focus could not be delivered to the page.
needsReview.howToConfirm: Every focusable element has a clearly visible focus indicator.

needsReview.title: Focus Not Obscured (Minimum)
needsReview.whatToReview: Check whether sticky header or overlays fully cover focused controls.
needsReview.whyOpen: No keyboard focus walk was available.
needsReview.howToConfirm: No focused control is entirely hidden by author content.

needsReview.title: Consistent Navigation
needsReview.whatToReview: Compare navigation order on at least three representative templates.
needsReview.whyOpen: A single page cannot establish consistency.
needsReview.howToConfirm: Repeated navigation appears in the same relative order.

needsReview.title: Consistent Identification
needsReview.whatToReview: Compare names and icons for components with identical functions.
needsReview.whyOpen: A single page cannot establish consistency.
needsReview.howToConfirm: The same function has the same label and identification across templates.

needsReview.title: Consistent Help
needsReview.whatToReview: Compare the placement of contact, help, or other assistance mechanisms across templates.
needsReview.whyOpen: A single page cannot establish consistency.
needsReview.howToConfirm: Repeated help mechanisms occur in the same relative order.
